message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="scott-wlaschin" color="green" summary="+1 Steve, focus management correctly deferred">
+1 on your observations, Steve. The empty state messaging and accessible labels are good.

On your point #4 (focus after delete): I noticed that Luca mentioned a deferred item was recorded for "focus management after HTMX delete for contexts." So this was identified and correctly deferred rather than ignored. That is the right process -- non-blocking a11y concerns go to `docs/deferred-items.md` and get reviewed at retros.

On point #5 (Cancel reloading the full page): Carson made the same observation. It is a minor inefficiency but works correctly. A dedicated fragment endpoint would be cleaner, and that could be addressed when we revisit contexts in a future step.

No additional concerns from my side on UX. The contexts management page is straightforward and does not make users think -- which is the goal.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Scott confirms alignment on my observations and notes the deferred item was properly tracked. No response needed — he's echoing what Carson and I already discussed, and he's given his consent on Kent's action items. Waiting for the remaining consents to close the retro.
